# Event Logger

**Auth**: 6-character secret code (per round, generated by admin) â†’ signed `rw_official_session` cookie
**Route prefix**: `/event-logger/events/[eventId]/...`
**Theme**: Dark

## What an event logger does

Records lap times and finish times during a round. The workspace runs one server-anchored race clock (elapsed from `round.startedAt`) and a fast click-driven grid: tap an athlete to capture their next split, or their finish when they cross on the final lap.

This is the only timing surface in the current build â€” the standalone "timekeeper" route no longer exists. The fast-tap recorder (`components/timekeeper/lap-recorder.tsx`) is shared by, and lives inside, this workspace, so the event logger is also the timekeeper. See [features/event-logging](../features/event-logging.md) and [features/timekeeping](../features/timekeeping.md).

| [event-logging](../features/event-logging.md) | Primary â€” lap + finish recording |

| [card-scoring](../features/card-scoring.md) | Cards appear in the round activity log (read-only here) |

## Pages visited

- [Workspace](../pages/event-logger/workspace.md)

## How it works

After the secret-code join (`joinAsOfficial`, [app/actions/officials.ts](../../app/actions/officials.ts)), the workspace renders the shared `LapRecorder` ([components/timekeeper/lap-recorder.tsx](../../components/timekeeper/lap-recorder.tsx)). Each tap calls `recordLapTime` or `recordFinishTime` in [app/actions/timing.ts](../../app/actions/timing.ts) â€” both require the `EVENT_LOGGER` position and an `ONGOING` round with a `startedAt`. `timeMs` is the elapsed race time captured client-side. Recording is idempotent (duplicate lap/finish is a no-op); finish positions are auto-assigned in order of crossing. After the last active athlete finishes, the round auto-ends. The page auto-refreshes via `AutoRefresh` (`router.refresh()` 2000ms while SCHEDULED, 2500ms otherwise).

## Typical workflow

1. Join via the 6-char code.
2. Wait for the admin to start the round (the race clock begins from `round.startedAt`).
3. As each athlete passes the lap line, tap their card:
   - Before the final lap â†’ records a lap split
   - On the final lap â†’ records their finish (position auto-assigned)
   - That athlete's button locks for a 10-second cooldown (a countdown ring shows the time left); others stay tappable
4. The round ends automatically once every active athlete has finished (or the admin ends it).
5. After the round, the activity log is the official record of timings + cards.
